name: payouts
invariants: output.ok === false || (output.result.payouts.reduce((a, x) => a + x.amountCc, 0) + output.result.unallocated === output.result.prizePoolCc)
_note: tournaments.payouts computes (or re-reads) the prize distribution on a completed tournament. Unknown id → 'tournament_not_found'; not completed → 'tournament_not_completed'. MONEY CONSERVATION is a hard invariant: distributed (sum of payout amountCc) + unallocated === prizePoolCc exactly — the pool is never over-allocated and never minted beyond the declared amount. A re-split with a poisoned weight (NaN/Infinity/1e308) is fail-CLOSED → 'invalid_payout_split' and the stored split is NOT mutated. (The not-found cases short-circuit before reaching split validation, hence the third fuzz case still returns 'tournament_not_found'.)
